conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I used to think remote work was the absolute pinnacle of software engineering culture. No commutes, total autonomy.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: It definitely has major perks for deep focus work.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But after managing a distributed team for two years, I realized isolation ruins collaboration. I became a hardcore advocate for 100% in-office environments. Real-time whiteboarding is irreplaceable.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: So you prefer everyone being in the physical office space now?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Well, forcing people in five days a week just causes burnout and resentment. I've landed on a firm conviction now: a structured hybrid model—exactly 3 days in office for collaborative design and 2 days remote for deep work—is the only sustainable way to build high-performing engineering teams.

query: What does the user believe is the best workplace model for engineering teams?
